docs.title: Branch Protection Rules
docs.text:
Branch protection rulesets applied to repositories in metapercept-metr.

Example repository: metR-Converter-FrameMaker Last updated: June 2026

docs.title: Ruleset overview
docs.text:
Ruleset ID (example) Target branches Secure Branch Protection Ruleset 17217760 dev, devops-testing metR-Phase2-Core-Governance 14869601 main, develop, staging, release/v*, feature/**, xslt/**, customer/**

Both rulesets are active and apply at the repository level. Organization admins can bypass in emergencies.

docs.title: Branch patterns
docs.text: Include Exclude refs/heads/dev — refs/heads/devops-testing —

docs.title: Rules
docs.text: Rule Effect creation Branch cannot be created outside allowed workflow update Direct pushes to branch are blocked deletion Branch cannot be deleted required_signatures All commits must be cryptographically signed and verified required_linear_history No merge commits — use squash or rebase non_fast_forward Force-push to protected branch blocked pull_request All changes must go through a PR

docs.title: Pull request parameters
docs.text: Setting Value Required approving reviews 2 Dismiss stale reviews on push Yes Require CODEOWNER review Yes Require approval of most recent push Yes Require resolved review threads Yes Allowed merge methods merge, squash, rebase

docs.title: Bypass
docs.text: Actor Mode Organization Admin Always

docs.title: Branch patterns
docs.text: Include Exclude refs/heads/main refs/heads/dependabot/** refs/heads/develop refs/heads/staging refs/heads/release/v* refs/heads/feature/** refs/heads/xslt/** refs/heads/customer/**

docs.title: Rules
docs.text: Rule Effect deletion Branch cannot be deleted non_fast_forward Force-push blocked required_linear_history Squash or rebase merge only pull_request All changes via PR required_status_checks CI must pass (see below)

docs.title: Pull request parameters
docs.text: Setting Value Required approving reviews 2 Dismiss stale reviews on push Yes Require CODEOWNER review Yes Require approval of most recent push Yes Require resolved review threads No Allowed merge methods merge, squash, rebase

docs.title: Required status checks (strict)
docs.text:
All checks must pass before merge. Branch must be up to date with base branch.

Check context Purpose unit-tests-pytest Python unit tests xslt-validation XSLT transformation validation dita-ot-schema-check DITA Open Toolkit schema validation security-scan Security / dependency scan

docs.title: Bypass
docs.text: Actor Mode Organization Admin Always

docs.title: Comparison matrix
docs.text: Requirement dev / devops-testing main / feature/** Signed commits Required Not in this ruleset 2 approving reviews Yes Yes CODEOWNER review Yes Yes Re-approve after push Yes Yes Resolve review threads Yes No Required CI checks No Yes (4 checks) Linear history Yes Yes Direct push Blocked Blocked

docs.title: Common merge blockers
docs.text: Error message Ruleset cause Fix Commits must have verified signatures required_signatures on dev Developer signs commits — see Signed Commits Cannot update this protected ref update rule + unmet requirements Satisfy all PR rules first Review required < 2 approvals or missing CODEOWNER Get 2 approvals incl. CODEOWNER Changes must be made through a pull request update / pull_request rules Do not push directly to protected branch Required status check expected Core Governance CI checks Fix failing CI on main / feature branches Review threads must be resolved required_review_thread_resolution on dev Resolve all conversations on PR

docs.title: Recommended merge method
docs.text: Target branch Recommended method Reason dev Squash and merge Linear history + single signed commit devops-testing Squash and merge Same as dev main Squash and merge Clean history; passes linear history rule

docs.title: Applying rulesets to a new repository
docs.text:
1. Go to Repository → Settings → Rules → Rulesets
  2. Verify both rulesets are inherited or assigned:
  3. Secure Branch Protection Ruleset
  4. metR-Phase2-Core-Governance
  5. Confirm branch patterns match your repo's branch naming
  6. Test with a sample PR before team rollout

docs.title: CODEOWNERS Standard
docs.text:
How CODEOWNERS files are managed across all metapercept-metr repositories.

Last updated: June 2026

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text: The CODEOWNERS file automatically requests reviews from designated owners when a pull request changes files they own. Combined with branch protection (require_code_owner_review: true), at least one CODEOWNER approval is mandatory before merge.

docs.title: File location
docs.text:
.github/CODEOWNERS

Must exist on each protected branch: dev, devops-testing, main (where the branch exists).

docs.title: Standard (default)
docs.text:
Use for all metR repos where both owners have write access.

File: templates/.github/CODEOWNERS

# This is a basic CODEOWNERS file.
# See https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners for details on how to specify code owners.

*  @nilesh-sk @naveenv-devops
Owner GitHub handle Role Nilesh @nilesh-sk Technical lead / CODEOWNER Naveen @naveenv-devops DevOps lead / CODEOWNER

The * pattern means both owners review all files in the repository.

docs.title: Restricted
docs.text:
Use when @nilesh-sk does not have collaborator write access on that repo.

File: templates/.github/CODEOWNERS.restricted

# This is a basic CODEOWNERS file.
# See https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/managing-your-repositorys-settings-and-features/customizing-your-repository/about-code-owners for details on how to specify code owners.

*  @naveenv-devops

docs.title: Validation rules
docs.text:
GitHub enforces these rules — our validation script checks them too.

Rule Description Write access required Every @user in CODEOWNERS must be a collaborator with write access Valid syntax File must parse without errors (GitHub API: codeowners/errors) Template match Content must match standard or restricted template exactly Correct path File must be at .github/CODEOWNERS

docs.title: Run validation (read-only)
docs.text:
py -3 scripts/validate_codeowners_org.py
Status Meaning valid + standard Both owners, GitHub accepts valid + restricted DevOps only, GitHub accepts invalid Fix before relying on CODEOWNER reviews skipped Branch does not exist

docs.title: Repositories using restricted template
docs.text:
Use restricted template when @nilesh-sk is intentionally not a collaborator:

Repository Reason KUBERNETES DevOps-only access oxygen-web-author DevOps-only access (others as decided by DevOps) No write access for @nilesh-sk

If you add @nilesh-sk as a collaborator later, upgrade to the standard template.

docs.title: How CODEOWNERS interacts with PR reviews
docs.text:
PR opened → dev branch
    │
    ├─ GitHub reads .github/CODEOWNERS on base branch (dev)
    ├─ Requests review from @nilesh-sk and @naveenv-devops
    │
    ├─ Branch protection requires:
    │     • 2 approving reviews total
    │     • At least 1 from a CODEOWNER
    │
    └─ Merge allowed when both conditions met (+ other rules)

docs.title: Adding CODEOWNERS to a new branch or repo
Manual (single repo)
docs.text:
1. Create .github/CODEOWNERS with the correct template
  2. Commit to dev, devops-testing, and/or main
  3. Run validation script

docs.title: Bulk rollout (org-wide)
docs.text:
py -3 scripts/add_codeowners_to_org.py

Write operation — requires gh CLI authenticated with repo scope.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Issue Cause Fix Unknown owner: @nilesh-sk User not a collaborator Add collaborator OR use restricted template CODEOWNER review not requested File missing on base branch Add .github/CODEOWNERS to target branch Approval doesn't count as CODEOWNER Approver not in CODEOWNERS file Only listed owners count content does not match template Extra whitespace or different owners Copy exact content from templates/

docs.title: Related documents
docs.text:
• GitHub Org Governance
  • Branch Protection Rules
  • PR Merge Guide

docs.title: GitHub Organization Governance
docs.text:
Standards that apply to all repositories in the metapercept-metr GitHub organization.

Organization: metapercept-metr DevOps owners: @naveenv-devops, @nilesh-sk Last updated: June 2026

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Every repository in the org follows a common governance model:

Area Standard Protected branches dev, devops-testing, main (+ feature/release branches per ruleset) CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS on all existing target branches Reviews Minimum 2 approvals; CODEOWNER review required Signed commits Required on dev and devops-testing Merge method Squash or rebase (linear history required) Direct pushes Blocked on protected branches — merge via PR only

docs.title: Standard branches
docs.text:
Branch Purpose Protection level main Production / stable release High (Core Governance ruleset) dev Active development integration High (Secure Branch Protection) devops-testing DevOps / pipeline validation High (Secure Branch Protection) develop, staging Environment promotion (where used) Core Governance ruleset feature/**, release/v* Feature and release work Core Governance ruleset

Not every repository has all three branches (dev, devops-testing, main). Branches are created as needed per project lifecycle.

docs.title: Category A — Full metR application repos
docs.text:
Examples: metR-Frontend, metR-API-Gateway, metR-Converter-FrameMaker, metR-Converter-DOCX

  • Branches: dev, devops-testing, main
  • CODEOWNERS: Standard (@nilesh-sk + @naveenv-devops)
  • Branch protection rulesets applied
  • CI status checks on governance branches (where configured)

docs.title: Category B — Infrastructure / tooling repos
docs.text:
Examples: metR-Core-Infrastructure, metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines, github-org-mcp-server, KUBERNETES

  • Branches: typically main only
  • CODEOWNERS: Standard or Restricted depending on collaborator access
  • DevOps-managed

docs.title: Category C — Website / admin panel repos
docs.text:
Examples: blog-metapercept, careers-metapercept, portfolio-metapercept, *-adminpanel-metapercept

  • Branches: main (some may lack dev / devops-testing)
  • CODEOWNERS: per-repo based on team access

docs.title: Category D — Test / demo repos
docs.text:
Examples: Asish_Test_Repo, Kajal_Test_Repo, demo-repository

  • Minimal governance; main branch only in most cases

docs.title: CODEOWNERS policy
docs.text:
Two approved templates — see CODEOWNERS Standard.

Template Owners When to use Standard @nilesh-sk @naveenv-devops Default for all metR repos Restricted @naveenv-devops only Repo where @nilesh-sk is not a collaborator

Rule: Every user listed in CODEOWNERS must have write access to that repository. GitHub rejects invalid owner entries.

File location: .github/CODEOWNERS Target branches: dev, devops-testing, main (where branch exists)

docs.title: Roles & responsibilities
docs.text: Role Person(s) Responsibilities Org admin DevOps lead Rulesets, bypass (emergency), org settings CODEOWNER @naveenv-devops, @nilesh-sk Approve PRs; ensure governance compliance Developer Feature branch author Signed commits, PR hygiene, resolve review threads Reviewer Any write-access member Code review; 2nd approval when required Merger CODEOWNER or admin Final merge after all rules pass

docs.title: Pull request workflow (summary)
docs.text:
Developer                    Reviewers / CODEOWNER           Merger
    │                              │                          │
    ├─ Create feature branch       │                          │
    ├─ Signed commits              │                          │
    ├─ Open PR → dev               │                          │
    │                              ├─ Review code             │
    │                              ├─ CODEOWNER approve       │
    │                              ├─ 2nd approval           │
    │                              ├─ Resolve all threads     │
    ├─ Fix & push (re-approval)    │                          │
    │                              │                          ├─ Squash/rebase merge
    │                              │                          └─ Into dev

Full guide: PR Merge Guide

docs.title: Branch protection rulesets
docs.text:
Two org-level rulesets are applied per repository. Details: Branch Protection Rules.

Ruleset Branches Key rules Secure Branch Protection dev, devops-testing Signed commits, 2 reviews, CODEOWNER, linear history metR-Phase2-Core-Governance main, feature/**, release/v*, etc. 2 reviews, CODEOWNER, required CI checks

docs.title: Validation & compliance
docs.text:
Run read-only validation after any CODEOWNERS change:

py -3 scripts/validate_codeowners_org.py
Result Meaning valid CODEOWNERS present, correct template, GitHub accepts owners invalid Missing file, wrong template, or owner lacks repo access skipped Branch does not exist in that repository

Reports are written to reports/codeowners-validation-latest.csv.

docs.title: New repository checklist
docs.text:
When creating a new repository in metapercept-metr:

  • [ ] Add .github/CODEOWNERS (standard or restricted template)
  • [ ] Create dev, devops-testing, main branches as needed
  • [ ] Apply Secure Branch Protection ruleset to dev / devops-testing
  • [ ] Apply metR-Phase2-Core-Governance ruleset to main / feature branches
  • [ ] Add required collaborators before listing them in CODEOWNERS
  • [ ] Run validate_codeowners_org.py to confirm
  • [ ] Document repo in team wiki / project board

docs.title: Related documents
docs.text:
• Branch Protection Rules
  • CODEOWNERS Standard
  • PR Merge Guide
  • Signed Commits Setup
  • Templates

Questions? Contact @naveenv-devops (DevOps lead).

docs.title: Pull Request Merge Guide
docs.text:
Step-by-step guide for opening, reviewing, and merging pull requests in metapercept-metr repositories.

Last updated: June 2026

docs.title: Roles
docs.text:
Role Who Responsibility PR author Developer (e.g. vaibhavsp-spec) Write code, sign commits, address review feedback CODEOWNER @naveenv-devops or @nilesh-sk Governance approval; required on every protected PR Reviewer Any team member with write access Code quality review; provides 2nd approval Merger CODEOWNER or org admin Final merge after all rules pass

The PR author fixes their own commits. The CODEOWNER approves and merges. An admin's SSH key does not sign another developer's commits.

docs.title: Step 1 — Author prepares branch
docs.text:
git checkout dev
git pull origin dev
git checkout -b feature/MDP-244-description

# Make changes with SIGNED commits
git commit -S -m "MDP-244: description of change"
git push -u origin feature/MDP-244-description

Signed commits are mandatory for dev and devops-testing. See Signed Commits Setup.

docs.title: Step 2 — Open pull request
docs.text: Field Value Base branch dev Compare branch feature/MDP-244-description Reviewers Request @naveenv-devops and one other reviewer Linked issue Jira / GitHub issue if applicable

docs.title: Step 3 — Review phase
docs.text:
Reviewers check:

  • [ ] Code quality and correctness
  • [ ] Tests pass locally / in CI
  • [ ] No secrets or credentials committed
  • [ ] Signed commits show Verified badge on GitHub

CODEOWNER checks:

  • [ ] CODEOWNERS compliance
  • [ ] Branch protection rules will be satisfied at merge time

docs.title: Step 4 — Merge requirements checklist (dev branch)
docs.text:
All items must be green before the merge button is enabled:

# Requirement Rule source 1 All commits verified signed required_signatures 2 2 approving reviews required_approving_review_count: 2 3 At least 1 CODEOWNER approval require_code_owner_review: true 4 Approvals are after the latest push require_last_push_approval: true 5 All review threads resolved required_review_thread_resolution: true 6 Branch is up to date with dev Best practice 7 Merge via Squash or Rebase required_linear_history

docs.title: Step 5 — Author fixes feedback
docs.text:
If changes are requested:

git add .
git commit -S -m "MDP-244: address review feedback"
git push

Important: Any new push dismisses existing approvals. Two fresh approvals are required after every push.

docs.title: Step 6 — Merge
docs.text:
Merger (@naveenv-devops or admin):

  1. Confirm all checklist items are green
  2. Click Squash and merge (recommended)
  3. Confirm commit message
  4. Delete feature branch (optional, recommended)

docs.title: Example — blocked PR resolution
docs.text:
Scenario: PR #14 on metR-Converter-FrameMaker — 2 approvals but merge blocked.

Blocker Owner Action Unsigned commits vaibhavsp-spec Set up signing → squash → signed force-push Approvals dismissed after push Reviewers 2 fresh approvals (incl. CODEOWNER) Unresolved threads Author + reviewers Resolve all conversations Merge @naveenv-devops Squash and merge into dev

docs.title: Merging into main / feature/** branches
docs.text:
Governed by metR-Phase2-Core-Governance ruleset. Additional requirements:

# Requirement 1 2 approving reviews (incl. CODEOWNER) 2 All 4 CI checks pass: unit-tests-pytest, xslt-validation, dita-ot-schema-check, security-scan 3 Branch up to date with base (strict policy) 4 Signed commits not required by this ruleset (but recommended)

docs.title: Emergency bypass (org admin only)
docs.text:
Organization admins can check "Merge without waiting for requirements to be met" to bypass rulesets.

Use only when:

  • Production incident requires immediate merge
  • Policy explicitly allows bypass
  • Bypass is logged and reviewed post-incident

docs.title: PR checklist card (copy for PR description)
docs.text:
## PR Checklist
- [ ] Commits are signed and show Verified on GitHub
- [ ] Branch is up to date with base branch
- [ ] Self-reviewed code changes
- [ ] Tests pass locally
- [ ] CODEOWNER reviewer requested
- [ ] All review threads resolved

docs.title: Related documents
docs.text:
• Branch Protection Rules
  • CODEOWNERS Standard
  • Signed Commits Setup
  • GitHub Org Governance

docs.title: Signed Commits Setup
docs.text:
dev and devops-testing branches require verified signed commits. This guide is for all developers in metapercept-metr.

Last updated: June 2026

docs.title: Why signed commits?
docs.text:
The Secure Branch Protection Ruleset enforces required_signatures on dev and devops-testing. Unsigned commits block PR merges with:

Commits must have verified signatures.

Each developer must sign their own commits. A CODEOWNER's SSH key on their laptop does not sign another person's work.

docs.title: Option A — SSH commit signing (recommended)
docs.text: Works well on Windows, macOS, and Linux. Uses your existing SSH key or a dedicated signing key.

docs.title: 1. Generate a signing key (or use existing)
docs.text: ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your-email@company.com" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing

docs.title: 2. Add public key to GitHub
docs.text:
1. Copy the public key: 
    cat ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing.pub
  2. GitHub → Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New SSH key
  3. Key type: Signing Key
  4. Paste public key and save

docs.title: 3. Configure Git
docs.text:
git config --global gpg.format ssh
git config --global user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing.pub
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

On Windows (Git Bash), the path may be: 

git config --global user.signingkey "C:/Users/YourName/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing.pub"

docs.title: 4. Verify
docs.text:
git commit --allow-empty -S -m "test signed commit"
git log --show-signature -1

Push to a test branch and confirm the Verified badge appears on GitHub.

docs.title: 1. Generate GPG key
docs.text:
gpg --full-generate-key
# Select: RSA and RSA, 4096 bits, your email

docs.title: 2. List key ID
docs.text:
gpg --list-secret-keys --keyid-format=long
# Copy the key ID after "rsa4096/"

docs.title: 3. Add to GitHub
docs.text:
gpg --armor --export YOUR_KEY_ID

GitHub → Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New GPG key → paste armored key.

docs.title: 4. Configure Git
docs.text:
git config --global user.signingkey YOUR_KEY_ID
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

docs.title: Fixing unsigned commits on an existing PR branch
docs.text: If your PR was opened before signing was set up, rewrite the branch with signed commits.

docs.title: Squash into one signed commit (simplest)
docs.text:
git checkout your-feature-branch
git fetch origin
git rebase origin/dev

git reset --soft origin/dev
git commit -S -m "MDP-XXX: description of all changes"
git push --force-with-lease

docs.title: Re-sign multiple commits (interactive rebase)
docs.text:
git rebase origin/dev
# Mark each commit for reword/edit and amend with -S:
git commit --amend -S --no-edit
git rebase --continue
git push --force-with-lease

After force-push, all PR approvals are dismissed. Reviewers must re-approve.

docs.title: Verify on GitHub
docs.text: Indicator Meaning Verified badge on commit Signing configured correctly No badge Commit is unsigned — merge will be blocked on dev Unverified badge Key not added to GitHub account

docs.title: Windows (Git Bash / MINGW64)
docs.text:
# Ensure ssh-agent is running
eval "$(ssh-agent -s)"
ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing

git config --global gpg.format ssh
git config --global user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing.pub
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

docs.title: VS Code
docs.text:
Enable in settings: 

"git.enableCommitSigning": true

docs.title: GitHub CLI commits
docs.text:
gh auth login
# Commits via gh pr merge use GitHub's signing when merged via web UI

docs.title: FAQ
docs.text: Question Answer Does my CODEOWNER need to sign my commits? No. You sign your own commits. Does squash merge create a signed commit? The merge commit is signed by GitHub if merged via web UI, but branch commits must still be signed for PR checks. I already have an SSH key for authentication Create a separate signing key or use the same key as a Signing Key in GitHub settings. Can admin bypass the signing rule? Org admins can bypass rulesets — emergency use only.

docs.title: Related documents
docs.text:
• PR Merge Guide
  • Branch Protection Rules
  • GitHub docs — Signing commits

Script Command Mode Validate CODEOWNERS py -3 scripts/validate_codeowners_org.py Read-only Rollout CODEOWNERS py -3 scripts/add_codeowners_to_org.py Write Org scan → XLSX py -3 scripts/generate_org_scan_xlsx.py Read-only

docs.title: Standard CI status checks
docs.text:
Required on metR-Phase2-Core-Governance branches (main, feature/**, etc.):

Context Purpose unit-tests-pytest Python unit tests xslt-validation XSLT transform validation dita-ot-schema-check DITA schema compliance security-scan Security / dependency scan

docs.title: Roles
docs.text:
Role Responsibility DevOps lead (@naveenv-devops) Pipelines, infra, org governance, releases CODEOWNER (@nilesh-sk) Technical review and approval Developers Signed commits, PR hygiene, CI green Org admin Ruleset bypass (emergency only)

Maintained by DevOps. Last updated: June 2026.

docs.title: CI/CD Overview
docs.text: Continuous integration and delivery practices for Met-R platform repositories.

docs.title: Pipeline model
docs.text:
Feature branch → PR → dev → devops-testing → main → Production
Stage Branch Gate Development feature/** CI checks + 2 reviews + CODEOWNER Integration dev Signed commits + 2 reviews + CODEOWNER + threads resolved DevOps validation devops-testing Same as dev Release main CI checks + 2 reviews + CODEOWNER

docs.title: GitHub Actions workflow
docs.text:
Each Met-R service repository includes workflows triggered on:

Event Action pull_request Run CI checks push to dev / main Run CI + deploy (per repo config) workflow_dispatch Manual deploy / validation

docs.title: Required status checks
docs.text:
On metR-Phase2-Core-Governance branches:

Check Description Failure action unit-tests-pytest Python unit test suite Fix tests before merge xslt-validation XSLT stylesheet validation Fix transform errors dita-ot-schema-check DITA OT schema validation Fix DITA structure security-scan Dependency / security scan Resolve vulnerabilities

Strict policy: branch must be up to date with base before merge.

docs.title: Shared pipelines
docs.text: Reusable workflow templates live in metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines. See Shared pipelines.

docs.title: Branch protection summary
docs.text:
Branch Signed commits CI checks CODEOWNER dev Required Optional Required devops-testing Required Optional Required main No Required Required feature/** No Required Required

Details: Branch protection

docs.title: Deployment triggers
docs.text: Environment Trigger Approved by Development Merge to dev CODEOWNER DevOps testing Merge to devops-testing DevOps Production Merge to main + release tag DevOps lead

docs.title: Related
docs.text:

docs.title: Environments
docs.text: Branch-to-environment mapping for Met-R platform services.

docs.title: Environment matrix
docs.text:
Environment Git branch Purpose Access Local Feature branch Developer workstation Developer Development dev Active integration Engineering team DevOps testing devops-testing Pipeline / infra validation DevOps Staging staging / develop Pre-production validation QA + leads Production main Live customer workloads Restricted

Not all repositories maintain every branch. See org scan report for branch availability per repo.

docs.title: Promotion flow
docs.text:
feature/MDP-XXX  →  dev  →  devops-testing  →  main
     (PR)           (PR)        (PR)            (release)

docs.title: Development (dev)
docs.text:
• Signed commits required
  • 2 approvals + CODEOWNER
  • All review threads resolved
  • Primary integration branch for feature work

docs.title: DevOps testing (devops-testing)
docs.text:
• Same protection as dev
  • Used for pipeline changes, infra validation, release candidates
  • DevOps-owned merges

docs.title: Production (main)
docs.text:
• 4 CI status checks required
  • 2 approvals + CODEOWNER
  • No signed commit requirement (by current ruleset)
  • Tagged releases from main

docs.title: Environment-specific configuration
docs.text: Config type Storage Managed by App settings Environment variables / Key Vault DevOps Secrets GitHub Secrets / Azure Key Vault DevOps Feature flags Server Admin / config service Platform team Pipeline vars GitHub Actions vars per environment DevOps

docs.title: DevOps & Engineering Tool Licensing
docs.text:
Licenses for development tooling used by the MetaPercept engineering team and metapercept-metr GitHub organization.

Last updated: June 2026

docs.title: Current license inventory
docs.text:
Tool Plan Seats Cost (monthly) Owner GitHub Team Team 15 users $60 ($4/user) DevOps GitHub Copilot Business 4 licenses $76 ($19/user) DevOps Met-R platform Enterprise (internal) — Internal Platform Cursor IDE Teams (if adopted) TBD TBD DevOps Cloud (Azure/AWS) Pay-as-you-go — Variable DevOps

Fixed tooling baseline: ~$136/month (GitHub Team + Copilot) + variable AI overages

docs.title: GitHub Team
docs.text: Item Detail Plan GitHub Team Organization metapercept-metr Users 15 Cost $4/user/month = $60/month Includes Private repos, GitHub Actions (2,000 min/month), branch protection, CODEOWNERS Billing github.com/pricing

• 52 repositories

docs.title: Engineer Onboarding
docs.text: Checklist for new engineers joining the Met-R platform team.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Complete before Day 1: DevOps prerequisites · Licensing

docs.title: Day 1 — Access
docs.text:
• [ ] GitHub org invite: metapercept-metr
  • [ ] Access to relevant repositories (based on team)
  • [ ] Met-R workspace credentials (if applicable)
  • [ ] Communication channels (Teams / Slack)

docs.title: Git & GitHub
docs.text:
# Git identity
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your-email@metapercept.com"

# Commit signing (REQUIRED for dev branch)
git config --global gpg.format ssh
git config --global user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing.pub
git config --global commit.gpgsign true

Add SSH signing key to GitHub → Settings → SSH keys → Signing Key.

Full guide: Signed commits

docs.title: GitHub CLI
docs.text:
gh auth login
gh auth status

docs.title: Day 2 — Understand the platform
docs.text: Read Time Platform architecture 30 min Services catalog 20 min CI/CD overview 15 min PR merge guide 15 min Key concepts (product) 15 min

docs.title: Day 3 — First contribution
docs.text:
1. Clone assigned service repository
  2. Create feature branch: feature/MDP-XXX-description
  3. Make a small change with signed commit
  4. Open PR to dev
  5. Request review from CODEOWNER (@naveenv-devops or @nilesh-sk)

docs.title: Branch rules reminder
docs.text: Branch Signed commits Reviews CI dev Yes 2 + CODEOWNER Optional main No 2 + CODEOWNER 4 checks

docs.title: Who to contact
docs.text: Topic Contact GitHub access @naveenv-devops Technical questions @nilesh-sk Platform architecture Platform lead

docs.title: DevOps Prerequisites
docs.text: Tools, access, and environment setup required for Met-R platform engineering.

docs.title: Access prerequisites
docs.text: Access Required for Request from GitHub org metapercept-metr All engineers @naveenv-devops Repository write access Service development DevOps lead GitHub org admin (select) Rulesets, bypass DevOps lead only Cloud portal (Azure/AWS) Infrastructure DevOps lead Kubernetes cluster Deployment DevOps lead Met-R staging workspace Integration testing Platform lead

docs.title: Required
docs.text: Tool Minimum version Purpose Git 2.40+ Version control GitHub CLI (gh) 2.40+ Org automation, API Python 3.11+ Converter services, scripts Node.js 20 LTS Frontend (metR-Frontend) Docker Desktop 4.x Local service development PowerShell / Bash — Scripting

docs.title: Recommended
docs.text: Tool Purpose kubectl Kubernetes management Terraform / Bicep Infrastructure as Code VS Code Primary IDE Postman / Insomnia API testing

docs.title: Git configuration (mandatory)
docs.text:
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "your-email@metapercept.com"
git config --global gpg.format ssh
git config --global user.signingkey ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing.pub

Signed commits are required for dev and devops-testing. See Signed commits.

docs.title: SSH keys
docs.text:
Key type Purpose GitHub setting Authentication key git push / gh SSH key Signing key Verified commits Signing key

Generate signing key: 

ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "your-email@metapercept.com" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_signing

docs.title: Python environment
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate   # Linux/macOS
# .venv\Scripts\activate    # Windows

pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt   # if present
pytest                                 # verify tests run

docs.text:
docker --version          # 24+
docker compose version    # v2
docker compose up -d      # per-service docker-compose.yml

docs.title: GitHub CLI authentication

gh auth login
# Select: GitHub.com → HTTPS or SSH → Login with browser

gh auth status
# Required scopes for DevOps scripts: repo, read:org, workflow

docs.title: Repository clone
docs.text:
gh repo clone metapercept-metr/metR-Converter-FrameMaker
cd metR-Converter-FrameMaker
git checkout dev
git pull origin dev

docs.title: CI local prerequisites
docs.text:
Before opening a PR, verify locally:

Check Command Unit tests pytest Linting ruff check . or project linter XSLT validation Project-specific script Signed commit git log --show-signature -1

CI runs these as required checks on main / feature/**: - unit-tests-pytest - xslt-validation - dita-ot-schema-check - security-scan

☐ GitHub org access granted
☐ Git identity configured
☐ SSH signing key added to GitHub
☐ gh CLI authenticated
☐ Python 3.11+ and Node 20 installed
☐ Docker running
☐ Repository cloned; on correct branch
☐ Tests pass locally

docs.title: Shared Pipelines
docs.text:
Reusable CI/CD workflow templates for Met-R services.

Repository: metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines Branch: main (CODEOWNERS: standard template)

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
Centralize pipeline logic so all converter, output, and platform services use consistent:

  • Test execution (unit-tests-pytest)
  • XSLT validation (xslt-validation)
  • DITA schema checks (dita-ot-schema-check)
  • Security scanning (security-scan)

docs.title: Consumption pattern
docs.text:
Service repositories reference shared workflows:

# .github/workflows/ci.yml (example pattern)
jobs:
  test:
    uses: metapercept-metr/metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines/.github/workflows/unit-tests.yml@main

docs.title: Available workflow templates
docs.text: Workflow Check context Services Unit tests unit-tests-pytest All Python services XSLT validation xslt-validation Converter services DITA schema dita-ot-schema-check DITA-related services Security scan security-scan All services

docs.title: Adding a new service to shared pipelines
docs.text:
1. Add service repo name to pipeline configuration in metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines
  2. Create .github/workflows/ci.yml in the new service referencing shared templates
  3. Enable required status checks in branch protection ruleset
  4. Verify checks appear on a test PR

docs.text:
• CI/CD overview
  • Platform — Infrastructure

docs.title: Runbooks
docs.text: Operational procedures for DevOps and platform engineering.

docs.title: Runbook index
docs.text: Runbook When to use Deployment Deploy a service to dev / production Incident response Production outage or degradation CODEOWNERS validation Audit CODEOWNERS across org Rollback Revert a bad deployment

docs.title: Severity definitions
docs.text: Level Description Response P1 Production down Immediate — all hands P2 Major feature broken < 4 hours P3 Degraded / workaround exists < 1 business day P4 Minor / planned maintenance Scheduled

docs.title: On-call contacts
docs.text:
Role Handle DevOps lead @naveenv-devops CODEOWNER @nilesh-sk

Expand runbooks as operational procedures mature.

docs.title: Runbook: CODEOWNERS Validation
docs.text: Audit CODEOWNERS compliance across the metapercept-metr organization.

docs.title: When to run
docs.text:
• After CODEOWNERS rollout or manual changes
  • Monthly compliance check
  • Before enabling new branch protection rules
  • After adding/removing collaborators

docs.title: Run validation (read-only)
docs.text: py -3 scripts/validate_codeowners_org.py

docs.title: Interpret results
docs.text:
Status Action valid + standard No action needed valid + restricted Expected for repos without @nilesh-sk access invalid Fix owner access or update CODEOWNERS template skipped Branch does not exist — no action unless branch planned

Report: reports/codeowners-validation-latest.csv

docs.title: Fix invalid entries
docs.text: Issue Fix Unknown owner @nilesh-sk Add collaborator OR switch to restricted template Missing file Run add_codeowners_to_org.py or add manually Wrong template Copy from templates/.github/CODEOWNERS

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• CODEOWNERS standard
  • Org governance

docs.title: Runbook: Deployment
docs.text: Deploy a Met-R service to a target environment.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• [ ] PR merged to target branch with all checks green
  • [ ] CODEOWNER approval recorded
  • [ ] Signed commits (if merging to dev)
  • [ ] Deployment window approved (production only)

docs.title: Deploy to development (dev)
docs.text:
1. Confirm PR merged to dev
  2. GitHub Actions deploy workflow triggers automatically
  3. Monitor workflow in Actions tab
  4. Verify service health endpoint
  5. Notify team in channel if breaking change

docs.title: Deploy to production (main)
docs.text:
1. Confirm PR merged to main with all 4 CI checks green
  2. Create release tag: vX.Y.Z
  3. Monitor deploy workflow
  4. Run smoke tests against production endpoints
  5. Update release notes
  6. Announce in team channel

docs.title: Verify deployment
docs.text: Check Command / action Service health Hit /health endpoint Logs Check observability dashboard API Gateway Test routed endpoint Frontend Load met-r.io UI

docs.title: Rollback
docs.text: If deployment fails, see Rollback runbook.

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Environments
  • CI/CD overview

docs.title: Runbook: Incident Response
docs.text: Respond to production incidents on the Met-R platform.

docs.title: Immediate actions (first 15 minutes)
docs.text:
1. Acknowledge — confirm incident in team channel
  2. Assess severity — P1 / P2 / P3 / P4
  3. Assign incident lead — typically DevOps on-call
  4. Check status — API Gateway, Frontend, affected converter service
  5. Communicate — notify stakeholders if P1/P2

docs.title: Investigation checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Recent deployments (last 2 hours)?
  • [ ] GitHub Actions failures?
  • [ ] Cloud provider status page?
  • [ ] Error rate spike in logs / monitoring?
  • [ ] Single service or platform-wide?

docs.title: Common failure points
docs.text: Symptom Likely cause Action API 502/503 Service pod down Restart / redeploy service Conversion jobs stuck Converter service unavailable Check converter pod logs Auth failures API Gateway / token issue Check gateway config CI pipeline red Test or validation failure Fix and redeploy

docs.title: Resolution
docs.text:
1. Apply fix or rollback (see Rollback)
  2. Verify health checks green
  3. Monitor for 30 minutes
  4. Post-incident: document root cause and preventive action

docs.title: Post-incident
docs.text:
• [ ] Update incident log
  • [ ] Schedule post-mortem if P1/P2
  • [ ] Create follow-up tickets

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Observability
  • Deployment runbook

docs.title: Runbook: Rollback
docs.text: Revert a failed deployment on the Met-R platform.

docs.title: When to rollback
docs.text:
• P1/P2 incident caused by recent deployment
  • Smoke tests fail after production deploy
  • Customer-reported regression tied to release

docs.title: Option A — Redeploy previous tag
docs.text:
1. Identify last known good tag: git tag --sort=-version:refname
  2. Trigger deploy workflow with previous tag via workflow_dispatch
  3. Verify health checks
  4. Notify team

docs.title: Option B — Revert merge commit on main
docs.text:
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git revert -m 1 <merge-commit-sha>
git push origin main
  1. CI runs on revert PR — ensure all checks pass
  2. Merge revert PR
  3. Verify production

docs.title: Option C — Hotfix branch
docs.text:
For targeted fix without full revert:

git checkout -b hotfix/MDP-XXX-fix main
# Apply minimal fix
git commit -S -m "hotfix: description"
# PR to main with expedited review

docs.title: Post-rollback
docs.text:
• [ ] Confirm incident resolved
  • [ ] Document root cause
  • [ ] Fix forward on dev before next release

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• Deployment runbook
  • Incident response

docs.title: Platform Architecture
docs.text: System design for the Met-R documentation orchestration platform.

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text: Principle Implementation Microservices Each converter/output service is independently deployable API-first All capabilities exposed via API Gateway Pipeline-driven Conversion and validation as automated pipelines Multi-tenant Workspace isolation per customer AI-augmented Optional AI agents layered on conversion workflows

docs.title: Request flow — document conversion
docs.text:
1. Client uploads document (UI or API)
2. API Gateway authenticates and routes to DocManager
3. DocManager stores metadata + file reference
4. Conversion job queued → appropriate Converter service
5. Converter runs transform + XSLT post-processing
6. Validation: xslt-validation, dita-ot-schema-check
7. Output service generates deliverable (PDF/HTML/XML)
8. DocPublisher routes to channel (DocSite, CMS, download)
9. Client notified (webhook / UI status update)

docs.title: Service communication
docs.text: Pattern Use case Synchronous REST API Gateway → service calls Async job queue Long-running conversions Shared storage Document files between services Event notification Job complete webhooks

docs.title: Admin planes
docs.text: Plane Component Scope Client metR-Client-Admin Workspace users, pipelines, client config Server metR-Server-Admin Infrastructure, service config, scaling

docs.title: Data boundaries
docs.text: Data type Isolation Retention Customer documents Per workspace Configurable Conversion logs Per workspace 90 days default Platform metrics Aggregated Per observability policy Secrets Key Vault / GH Secrets Rotated per policy

Artifact Registry Orchestration Container image Container registry Kubernetes Configuration Key Vault / GH Secrets Per environment Pipeline GitHub Actions Per repository

docs.title: Platform Prerequisites
docs.text: Infrastructure and technical requirements for deploying and operating the Met-R platform.

docs.title: Deployment model prerequisites
docs.text: Model Prerequisites SaaS (multi-tenant) MetaPercept-managed; customers need browser + account only Dedicated cloud Customer contract; MetaPercept provisions isolated instance Private / on-prem Customer provides infrastructure below

docs.title: Kubernetes cluster
docs.text:
Requirement Minimum Recommended Kubernetes version 1.27+ 1.29+ Worker nodes 3 5+ (across AZs) Node CPU 4 vCPU per node 8 vCPU Node RAM 16 GB per node 32 GB Storage class SSD-backed PV NVMe / premium SSD Ingress controller NGINX or cloud LB With TLS termination DNS Managed zone for *.met-r.io

Repository: KUBERNETES — manifests and Helm charts

docs.title: Cloud provider
docs.text: Resource Specification Compute Managed Kubernetes (AKS, EKS, or GKE) Object storage S3-compatible or Azure Blob for document files Container registry ACR, ECR, or GHCR Secrets Azure Key Vault or AWS Secrets Manager Load balancer Cloud-native L7 LB with TLS DNS Route53, Azure DNS, or Cloudflare

docs.title: Core infrastructure repository
docs.text:
Repository: metR-Core-Infrastructure

Deploy order: 1. Networking (VNet/VPC, subnets, NSGs) 2. Kubernetes cluster 3. Container registry 4. Key Vault / secrets 5. Storage accounts 6. Ingress and TLS certificates 7. Platform namespaces and services

docs.title: Per-service resource guidelines
docs.text:
Service type CPU request Memory request Replicas (min) API Gateway 500m 512 Mi 2 Frontend 250m 256 Mi 2 Converter service 1000m 1 Gi 2 DocManager 500m 512 Mi 2 Output service 1000m 1 Gi 2 AI Agent 2000m 2 Gi 1

Scale horizontally based on queue depth and CPU (HPA configured per service).

docs.title: Network prerequisites
docs.text: Rule Detail Ingress HTTPS 443 from internet to API Gateway + Frontend Internal Service mesh / cluster DNS for inter-service calls Egress Outbound HTTPS for AI APIs, package registries Firewall Deny all except required ports TLS Valid certificate for custom domain

docs.title: Database & storage
docs.text: Component Requirement Document metadata DB PostgreSQL 14+ (managed) Job queue Redis or cloud message queue File storage Object storage; min 500 GB (production) Backup Daily automated backup; 30-day retention

docs.title: Identity & SSO (enterprise)
docs.text: Requirement Detail IdP SAML 2.0 or OIDC (Okta, Entra ID, etc.) SCIM Optional — automated user provisioning MFA Enforced on IdP for admin accounts

docs.title: Monitoring prerequisites
docs.text:
Component Requirement Metrics Prometheus-compatible or cloud monitor Logs Centralized aggregation (90-day retention) Alerts Pager / Teams integration for P1/P2 Health endpoints /health on all services

See Observability.

docs.title: CI/CD prerequisites
docs.text: Component Requirement GitHub Actions Org-level runners or self-hosted runners Shared pipelines metR-Phase2-shared-pipelines access Container build Docker buildx; push to registry Secrets in CI GitHub Secrets per environment

docs.title: Security prerequisites
docs.text:
Control Requirement Pod security Non-root containers, read-only filesystem where possible Network policies Namespace isolation Image scanning Scan on push to registry Secrets Never in code; Key Vault / GH Secrets only Branch protection Active on all service repos

See Security & compliance.

docs.title: Pre-deployment checklist
docs.text:
☐ Kubernetes cluster provisioned (1.27+)
☐ Container registry accessible from cluster
☐ Key Vault / secrets manager configured
☐ Object storage bucket created
☐ PostgreSQL instance provisioned
☐ TLS certificate issued for domain
☐ DNS records configured
☐ Ingress controller deployed
☐ Monitoring and alerting connected
☐ GitHub Actions deploy workflow tested
☐ metR-Core-Infrastructure applied
☐ Smoke test passed on staging

docs.title: Security & Compliance
docs.text: Platform-level security controls for Met-R.

docs.title: Security layers
docs.text: Layer Control Transport TLS 1.2+ on all external traffic Authentication API keys, OAuth, workspace SSO (enterprise) Authorization RBAC per workspace; CODEOWNERS on repos Secrets Key Vault / GitHub Secrets; never in source code Supply chain security-scan on every PR to governance branches Branch protection Signed commits, required reviews, no direct push

docs.title: Repository security
docs.text:
Control Implementation CODEOWNERS .github/CODEOWNERS on all protected branches Branch rulesets Secure Branch Protection + Core Governance Signed commits Required on dev / devops-testing Dependency scanning security-scan CI check Org admin bypass Emergency only; logged

Details: GitHub org governance

docs.title: Data security
docs.text: Data type Protection Customer documents Workspace isolation; encrypted at rest API tokens Scoped per workspace; rotatable Audit logs Admin actions logged in Client/Server Admin PII Minimize collection; configurable retention

docs.title: Compliance readiness
docs.text: Area Status Access control RBAC + CODEOWNERS Change management PR + review + CI gates Audit trail GitHub audit log + platform logs Vulnerability management security-scan CI check Formal certifications Contact MetaPercept for SOC/ISO docs

docs.title: Security incident process
docs.text:
1. Report to @naveenv-devops
  2. Follow Incident response runbook
  3. Assess data exposure
  4. Remediate and document
